title: dioclea lasiophylla
intro: Dioclea lasiophylla, also known as the "pigeon pea" or "red gram," is a type of legume plant native to tropical regions of Central and South America. Dioclea lasiophylla has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of various compounds such as flavonoids and saponins. The plant has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and seeds of Dioclea lasiophylla are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
